name: agent-md-refactor
description: Refactor bloated AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or similar agent instruction files to follow progressive disclosure principles. Splits monolithic files into organized, linked documentation.
capabilities: Analyzes agent instruction files for contradictions and conflicts
Identifies essential instructions to keep in the root file
Groups remaining instructions into logical categories
Creates organized file structure with linked documentation
Flags redundant or vague instructions for deletion
author: softaworks
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation-only skill containing SKILL.md and README.md files. The scanner flagged markdown code examples (backtick syntax) and text patterns as security issues, but these are simply documentation content with no executable code.
valueStatement: Claude Code often accumulates hundreds of lines of mixed instructions in AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md files, wasting context tokens and creating maintenance headaches. This skill refactors monolithic agent instructions into clean, linked documentation using progressive disclosure principles.
limitations: Does not modify files without user approval
Does not execute any code or shell commands
Does not access external APIs or networks
Does not handle non-markdown instruction formats

useCases.title: Clean up overgrown CLAUDE.md files
useCases.description: When a project's CLAUDE.md has grown to 200+ lines with mixed topics, this skill restructures it into a minimal root file plus focused linked documents.

useCases.title: Resolve conflicting agent instructions
useCases.description: Before restructuring, the skill identifies contradictory guidelines (like 'use semicolons' vs 'no semicolons') and asks the user to resolve them first.

useCases.title: Prepare new projects with organized instructions
useCases.description: For new projects, the skill provides templates and structure for organizing agent instructions from the start using progressive disclosure.

promptTemplates.title: Basic refactoring request
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor my CLAUDE.md file to follow progressive disclosure principles. Keep only essential instructions in the root and organize the rest into linked files.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to clean up a bloated agent instruction file

promptTemplates.title: Split long AGENTS.md
promptTemplates.prompt: My AGENTS.md file is too long and mixed with unrelated topics. Analyze it for contradictions, then split it into a minimal root file plus categorized linked documentation.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has a long, disorganized AGENTS.md file

promptTemplates.title: Organize agent config
promptTemplates.prompt: Organize my agent configuration. Identify what belongs in the root file versus linked files. Create a file structure with proper links and templates.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants systematic organization of instructions

promptTemplates.title: Full refactoring with deletion
promptTemplates.prompt: Completely refactor my agent instructions. Find all contradictions, identify essentials vs optional content, create categorized linked files, and flag anything that should be deleted for wasting context tokens.

bestPractices: Commit current state before refactoring so you can review changes as a diff
Resolve all contradictions before proceeding to avoid propagating conflicts
Keep the root file under 50 lines - move anything not universal to linked files
Delete vague or redundant instructions rather than keeping them
antiPatterns: Keeping everything in the root file even when most instructions are rarely used
Creating too many small files that fragment the documentation
Including obvious or default behaviors that the agent already knows
Not testing the refactored structure with actual tasks to verify it works

faq.question: Does this skill modify my files directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes your files and generates a refactoring plan with templates. You review and apply the changes yourself.

faq.question: How long should my root file be after refactoring?
faq.answer: Aim for under 50 lines. The root should only contain instructions that apply to 100% of tasks, like project description and build commands.

faq.question: How many linked files should I create?
faq.answer: Target 3-8 linked files. Too few creates bloated files, too many creates navigation overhead. Group related topics together.

faq.question: What instructions should I delete?
faq.answer: Delete redundant instructions (agent already knows), vague instructions (not actionable), obvious advice (don't introduce bugs), and outdated references.

faq.question: Can I use this for COPILOT.md or other formats?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with any agent instruction file format including CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, COPILOT.md, and similar.

faq.question: How do I verify the refactoring worked?
faq.answer: Test with typical tasks to ensure the agent can find needed instructions. Check that all links work and no important instructions were lost.
